The forest engineer, formerly head of the Forestry Section of the Canton of Ticino and now retired, expressed enthusiasm for his new role: 'The world of trails and hiking has accompanied me throughout my life, both professionally and privately. I feel that it is time to dedicate more time to this important heritage.' David succeeds Stéphane Grounauer, who will take on the role of operational director from 1 August. Grounauer commented: 'During the past year, the association has had to face some staffing challenges, and after discussing with the committee, this unexpected proposal emerged: to personally take on the operational management. A challenge I accept with a great sense of responsibility and enthusiasm for a world that I am enormously passionate about.'

### New committee members
The assembly also approved the election of two new committee members: Fabienne Lanini, project manager at the SILVIVA foundation, and Andrea Stella, director of the Gole della Breggia Park. Mattia Soldati, on the other hand, leaves after 8 years in his role on the association's committee. He and Ilaria Jam, former administrative manager and secretary for 15 years, were appointed as honorary members of the association for their appreciated work and commitment to the cause of hiking.

### Approval of the 2025 financial report
The traditional gathering also allowed to review the main stages of TicinoSentieri's commitment over the past year and to approve the 2025 financial report. Samuele Patelli, representing the mobility section of the Department of Territory, presented some data on the reconstruction work carried out thanks to the Framework Credit 2024-2027, amounting to CHF 5 million. Among the most significant projects, the reconstruction of the Alpino trail between Acquarossa and Lottigna, 7 km long, and the restoration of the Monte Generoso trail, which saw a 30% increase in visitors in 2025 compared to the previous year.

### Operational checklist for 2026
For 2026, TicinoSentieri has planned a series of targeted interventions:
- Extraordinary maintenance: 15 priority trails, including the Lago di Lugano trail and the Monte San Salvatore route.
- Safety: Installation of 50 new informative signs and luminous signage in high-risk areas, such as the Monte Tamaro trail.
- Accessibility: Creation of accessible paths for disabled people in 3 locations, including the Lago di Lugano trail.

### Comparisons between practical scenarios
The association highlighted how regular maintenance of the trails can reduce reconstruction costs by 40%. For example, the Lago di Lugano trail, which has undergone annual maintenance, required only 10% of the budget planned for reconstruction in 2025. On the contrary, the Monte Generoso trail, which has not received regular maintenance, required 70% of the planned budget.

### Regulations and financing
The assembly also discussed the regulations in force, such as the Federal Law on Hiking of 2023, which provides for federal contributions for the maintenance of the trails. TicinoSentieri has received CHF 1 million for 2026, which will be used for the ordinary and extraordinary maintenance of the trails.

### Discussion moment
At the end of the evening, there was an appreciated moment of discussion about the history of Ticino's trails thanks to the knowledge of historian and journalist Jonas Marti. Marti illustrated the evolution of Ticino's trails from the 19th century, highlighting how the trail network has gone from 500 km in 1850 to over 2,000 km in 2026. ### Implications for cross-border workers
The new appointments in TicinoSentieri could have a significant impact on cross-border workers who use Ticino's trails for excursions and outdoor activities. With Roland David, who has a long political career at the municipal and cantonal level, at the helm of the association, a greater focus on the maintenance and development of trails is expected, thus improving the experience for hikers. For example, the Monte Tamaro trail, frequently used by cross-border workers from Lugano and Mendrisio, could benefit from targeted interventions to ensure greater safety and accessibility. Furthermore, the association plans to expand the offer of marked trails, with an investment of approximately 500,000 CHF in the 2026-2028 triennium, funded by the 2024-2027 Framework Credit.

### Comparisons with the previous situation
Before the new appointments, the association had faced some staffing challenges. With Stéphane Grounauer taking on the role of operational director, greater efficiency in the management of daily activities is expected. This could translate into better maintenance of the trails and a more varied offer of hiking routes. For example, the Lago Maggiore trail, which connects the municipalities of Locarno and Ascona, could be enhanced with new signage and rest stops, thanks to a dedicated budget of 200,000 CHF. Moreover, the introduction of a digital monitoring system could reduce intervention times in case of need, as already happened in 2025 with the San Gottardo trail, where response times were reduced by 30%.

### Future scenarios
With the approval of the 2025 Financial Statement and the reconstruction works funded by the 2024-2027 Framework Credit, a period of development is expected for TicinoSentieri. The new committee members, Fabienne Lanini and Andrea Stella, will bring new skills and perspectives, contributing to a more dynamic future for the association. Honorary members Mattia Soldati and Ilaria Jam will continue to support the association, ensuring continuity in the commitment to hiking. For example, the reconstruction project of the Monte Generoso trail, funded with 300,000 CHF, could be completed by 2026, thanks to the collaboration between the new committee members and local technicians.

### Operational checklist
- **Routine maintenance**: Periodic check of main trails (e.g., Monte Tamaro trail, Lago Maggiore).
- Safety: Installation of new signage and rest stops.
- Digital monitoring: Implementation of a monitoring system to reduce intervention times.
- Funding: Use of funds from the 2024-2027 Framework Credit for specific projects (e.g., Monte Generoso trail).

### Comparisons between practical scenarios
Before the new appointments, the maintenance of the trails was entrusted to volunteers and local technicians, with longer intervention times and limited resources. With the introduction of an operational director and a digital monitoring system, a 30% reduction in intervention times and a significant improvement in the quality of the trails are expected. For example, the San Gottardo trail has already benefited from these changes, with a 20% reduction in maintenance times in 2025.

### Regulations and funding
The new appointments and development projects are in line with cantonal regulations on hiking and environmental protection. The 2024-2027 Framework Credit, approved by the Grand Council of Ticino, provides funding of 1.5 million CHF for the maintenance and development of trails. Furthermore, the association has obtained additional funding of 500,000 CHF for specific projects, such as the reconstruction of the Monte Generoso trail.

### Conclusions
The new appointments in TicinoSentieri represent a significant opportunity to improve the hiking experience for cross-border workers and residents. With a greater focus on maintenance, safety, and innovation, the association is ready to face future challenges with greater efficiency and dynamism. The ongoing projects and those planned for 2026-2028 promise to transform Ticino's trails into a model of excellence for hiking.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### What to do for cross-border workers
For cross-border workers who wish to enjoy Ticino's trails, it is essential to stay updated on the news and initiatives from TicinoSentieri. Visit the official website of the association for detailed information on the trails, upcoming events, and the latest legislative news. For example, since 2023, cross-border workers can benefit from tax incentives for excursions in Ticino, with a 20% rebate on trail maintenance costs, up to a maximum of 500 CHF per year.

Participating in events organized by TicinoSentieri is a great way to get to know the trails better and contribute to their maintenance. These events are often free or low-cost and offer the opportunity to explore lesser-known paths, such as the Monte Generoso trail, which stretches for 12 km and offers spectacular views of Lake Lugano.

### Procedure to participate in events
1. **Visit the official TicinoSentieri website**: Here you can find all the necessary information about the trails and events.
2. Consult the event calendar: The calendar is updated monthly and includes details on dates, times, and locations of events.
3. Register for events of interest: Registration is simple and can be done online. Some events require a symbolic participation fee.
4. Participate in events and contribute to trail maintenance: During the events, participants can contribute to the maintenance of the trails, such as cleaning the Monte San Salvatore trail, which requires about 2 hours of volunteer work.

### Useful tools
Use the route calculator to plan your excursions and discover the trails best suited to your needs. For example, the Monte Tamaro trail, which is 10 km long, is ideal for half-day excursions. Consult the trail map to explore the available routes and plan your excursions. The map includes details on difficulty, duration, and points of interest along the route, such as the Alpine Refuge of Alpe di Neggia, located at an altitude of 1,618 meters.

### Operational checklist
- Check local regulations for cross-border workers, such as the 2023 regulation that provides tax incentives.
- Plan your excursion using the route calculator and the trail map.
- Register for events of interest through the official TicinoSentieri website.
- Bring appropriate equipment, such as hiking shoes and a backup paper map.
- Check the weather forecast before you leave.

### Comparison of practical scenarios
- Individual excursion: Plan a day-long excursion on the Monte Brè trail, which offers panoramic views of Lugano. The duration is about 3 hours and does not require special equipment.
- Group event: Participate in a maintenance event on the Monte Generoso trail, which requires about 4 hours of volunteer work and offers the opportunity to meet other hiking enthusiasts.

> "Maintaining the trails is fundamental to preserving the natural beauty of Ticino and making it accessible to everyone." - TicinoSentieri

### Regulations and incentives
Since 2023, cross-border workers can benefit from tax incentives for excursions in Ticino. These incentives include a 20% rebate on trail maintenance costs, up to a maximum of 500 CHF per year. To benefit from these incentives, you need to submit an online application on the official TicinoSentieri website and provide proof of the expenses incurred.

### Conclusion
Staying updated on TicinoSentieri's initiatives and participating in trail maintenance events is an excellent way for cross-border workers to explore and contribute to the natural beauty of Ticino. Using the available tools, such as the route calculator and the trail map, can make your excursions more enjoyable and safe.

*Source: [ticinonews.ch](https://www.ticinonews.ch/ticino/un-nuovo-presidente-e-un-nuovo-direttore-ticinosentieri-si-rinnova-432619)*

item.title: Como: 10 students become local police officers with On the Road project
item.description: A three-year agreement between the City of Como, the Local Police, and the On the Road association to train young officers
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- 10 students from 5 schools become local police officers in Como
- Three-year project with the association Ragazzi On the Road
- Practical experience with the Local Police of Como

## Key facts
- **What**: On the Road educational project
- When: Three-year agreement, starts April 28, 2026
- Where: Local Police Command of Como, viale Innocenzo XI
- Who: Municipality of Como, Local Police, Ragazzi On the Road Association
- Amount: 10 students involved

Como chooses to transform prevention from an isolated initiative into a pillar of civic growth. This is the value of the three-year agreement signed between the Municipality of Como, the Local Police Command, and the association Ragazzi On the Road, which brings to the city 'On the Road', a consolidated educational model at the national level, which is revolutionizing the relationship between institutions and new generations.

A project that has already received significant recognition even at the institutional level: during the last municipal council, the mayor Alessandro Rapinese, the President of the Municipal Council Fulvio Anzaldo, and the entire Council expressed their praise to the young participants, underlining the educational value of the initiative and its impact on the territory. An important institutional moment, shared in the presence of the development manager of the association Ragazzi On the Road Marco Zanchi and the staff manager Riccardo Bonzi.

### The inaugural day

The inaugural day, held at the Local Police Command of viale Innocenzo XI on Tuesday, April 28, is the first application: a group of 10 students has started a path that in the coming years will be progressively expanded, consolidating the bond between young people and institutions.

Present were, for the Municipal Administration, the Deputy Mayor Nicoletta Roperto; for the Local Police Command the Commander Vincenzo Aiello and the Deputy Commander Sergio D'Angelo; for the association Ragazzi On the Road the Vice President Alessandro Invernici, Marco Zanchi (development manager) and the organizational staff.

### Statements from the authorities

'The three-year agreement signed with the Municipality of Como and the Local Police Command represents a clear and far-sighted choice: investing in young people not symbolically, but through concrete experiences of on-field responsibility' declared Alessandro Invernici, founder of the project and Vice President of the association.

The value of this start lies precisely in the will of the Command and the Administration to open up, to share skills and operational daily life with the new generations. The most effective prevention is born here: from direct knowledge, mutual trust, and the possibility, for the boys, to really understand what it means to guarantee security and manage emergencies. This first group is only the beginning of a path that, thanks to the three-year agreement, will become an integral part of the life of the city'.

'On the Road' was born precisely with this objective: to allow young people to experience from the inside the role of institutions, law enforcement, and rescuers, developing awareness, responsibility, and civic sense through direct experience.

### The activities of the young agents

The first ten young people, involved from April 28 to May 4, come from the institutes I.I.S. 'Leonardo da Vinci – Ripamonti' of Como, I.T.I.S. 'Magistri Cumacini' of Como, I.I.S. 'G. Bertacchi' of Lecco, CFP 'Giuseppe Terragni' and Institute 'Matilde di Canossa'. During this week they will assist the Local Police in operational activities: road checks, evening services, canine units, and checks on heavy vehicles, in addition to learning the internal functions of the Command.

The path will ### Implications for Youth and the Community

The On the Road project represents a unique opportunity for young people in Como to approach the world of safety and prevention. Through this experience, students can acquire practical skills and develop a sense of civic responsibility.

### Comparison with Similar Initiatives

Unlike other educational initiatives, On the Road offers a direct and concrete experience, allowing young people to work closely with law enforcement. This practical approach is fundamental for the formation of aware and responsible citizens.

### Future Scenarios

With the three-year agreement, the On the Road project has the potential to become a reference model for other cities. The goal is to increase the number of students involved and to extend the project at the regional level.

### Impact on the Territory

The initiative has a positive impact not only on the participating youth but also on the local community. The presence of young local police officers contributes to strengthening the sense of security and belonging to the community.

### School Involvement

The schools involved in the project represent an important part of Como's educational fabric. The participation of students from different schools fosters collaboration between educational institutions and the Local Police.

### Skill Development

Through the On the Road project, students have the opportunity to develop cross-cutting skills, such as teamwork, emergency management, and effective communication. These skills are fundamental for their personal and professional growth.

### Collaboration with Institutions

The collaboration between the Municipality of Como, the Local Police, and the Ragazzi On the Road association represents a virtuous example of synergy between institutions. This type of collaboration is essential for the success of educational and prevention projects.

### Direct Experiences

Direct experience with the Local Police allows young people to better understand the role of law enforcement and to develop greater respect for rules and institutions. This practical approach is fundamental for the formation of aware and responsible citizens.

### Future Developments

The On the Road project has the potential to become a reference model for other cities and regions. The goal is to increase the number of students involved and to extend the project at the national level.

### Conclusion

In conclusion, the On the Road project represents a unique opportunity for young people in Como to approach the world of safety and prevention. Through this experience, students can acquire practical skills and develop a sense of civic responsibility. The initiative has a positive impact not only on the participating youth but also on the local community, strengthening the sense of security and belonging to the community.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### What to do to participate

If you are a student interested in participating in the On the Road project, you can contact your school or the Ragazzi On the Road association for more information. The schools involved in the project are I.I.S. “Leonardo da Vinci – Ripamonti” in Como, I.T.I.S. “Magistri Cumacini” in Como, I.I.S. “G. Bertacchi” in Lecco, CFP “Giuseppe Terragni” and the “Matilde di Canossa” Institute.

### Enrollment procedure

To enroll in the project, you need to fill out a registration form available at the participating schools or on the Ragazzi On the Road association’s website. The form must be submitted to the school’s secretariat or sent via email to the address indicated on the website.

### Participation requirements

The requirements to participate in the On the Road project include a minimum age of 16 years and attendance at one of the participating schools. Students must also be available to participate in the activities planned by the project, which will take place during the indicated period.

### Planned activities

The activities planned for the On the Road project include road checks, evening services, canine units, and checks on heavy vehicles. Students will have the opportunity to work closely with the Local Police, acquiring practical skills and developing a sense of civic responsibility.

### Deadlines and important dates

Important dates for the On the Road project include the start of activities on April 28, 2026, and the end of activities on May 4, 2026. Interested students must enroll by April 20, 2026, to participate in the project.

### Useful contacts

For more information about the On the Road project, you can contact the Ragazzi On the Road association at the phone number or email address indicated on the website. You can also visit the association’s website for further details about the project and the planned activities.

### Useful tools

To calculate your salary and better understand the opportunities offered by the On the Road project, you can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator). This tool will help you better understand the opportunities offered by the project and plan your future.

### Conclusion

The On the Road project represents a unique opportunity for young people in Como to approach the world of safety and prevention. Through this experience, students can acquire practical skills and develop a sense of civic responsibility. If you are interested in participating, contact your school or the Ragazzi On the Road association for more information.

*Source: [comozero.it](https://comozero.it/attualita/como-10-studenti-di-5-scuole-diventano-agenti-di-polizia-locale-ecco-il-progetto-on-the-road/)*

item.title: Como Cold Weather Plan: 200 people hosted for 172 nights
item.description: Como's Cold Weather Plan has hosted 200 homeless people for 172 nights, thanks to the collaboration of volunteers and donors.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Piano Freddo Como: 200 people welcomed
- 172 nights of winter accommodation
- 550 volunteers and 20,000 euros raised

## Key facts
- **What**: Winter accommodation for homeless people
- When: From 1st December 2025 to 1st May 2026
- Where: Como and surroundings
- Who: Vicini di Strada, Como Municipality, Como Province
- Amount: 200 people welcomed, 550 volunteers, 20,000 euros raised

The Piano Freddo of Como concluded with a positive balance: 200 homeless people found shelter for 172 nights thanks to the initiative that started on 1st December 2025. This project, carried out thanks to the collaboration between Vicini di Strada, the Planning Office of the Como Territorial Area, the Municipality of Como and the Province of Como, offered vital support during the winter months. The protection network was articulated in various structures and projects, including the Dormitory of via Borgovico, the Betlemme Project of the Caritas Foundation, the Dormitory of via Napoleona and the Piccola Casa Federico Ozanam. Each structure welcomed a significant number of people, with a total of 96 unique presences at the Dormitory of via Borgovico, 27 people at the Betlemme Project, 69 people at the Dormitory of via Napoleona and 2 admissions to the Piccola Casa Federico Ozanam.

### The heart of the project
The implementation of the Piano Freddo was made possible thanks to the indispensable contribution of about 550 volunteers, 400 in the parishes of the Betlemme Project and 150 at the Winter Dormitory of Via Borgovico. Extraordinary also the response of the citizens: the fundraising campaign reached almost 20,000 euros. 'This experience fits into a broader path aimed at improving the quality of life of those who find themselves in conditions of severe marginality, promoting an active and aware citizenship to guarantee everyone a dignified daily life', explains the note.

### How to support the network
To support the initiatives and projects of the network, the Vicini di Strada Fund is active on the crowdfunding platform of the Fondazione Provinciale della Comunità Comasca. Vicini di Strada includes various local associations and cooperatives, including ASCI Don Guanella, Associazione Como Accoglie, Associazione Comunità Il Gabbiano ODV, Associazione Incroci, Associazione Lachesi, Piccola Casa Federico Ozanam ODV, Caritas cittadine, Cooperativa Sociale Lavoro e Solidarietà, Cooperativa sociale Symploké, Cooperativa sociale Lotta contro l’emarginazione, Croce Rossa Italiana Comitato di Como, CSV Insubria, Fondazione Caritas Solidarietà e Servizio, Fondazione Somaschi, Gruppo 'Legami', Opera don Guanella, Osservatorio giuridico per i diritti dei migranti and Parrocchia di Rebbio.

### Entities and volunteer groups
The entities and volunteer groups of the Piano Freddo include Fondazione Somaschi (Borgovico managing entity), Fondazione Caritas Solidarietà e Servizio, ACLI, ANA-Civil Protection, Associazione Como Accoglie, Associazione Incroci, Comunità di famiglie alle Querce di Mamre, Croce Rossa Italiana, Gruppo 'Legami', Gruppo 'Elleboro', Nuovi Orizzonti, Ordine Francescano Secolare, Parrocchie di Como e dintorni, Caritas cittadine, Civil Protection of Cantù, Civil Protection of Olgiate Comasco, Scout Agesci and many individual citizens and citizens of Como. - ### Implications for cross-border workers
The Como Cold Plan represents a model of solidarity that could have implications for cross-border workers who work in Switzerland and reside in Italy. The collaboration between local entities and the community demonstrates how effective support networks can be created to address winter emergencies. For cross-border workers, this type of initiative could offer an example of how communities can come together to ensure assistance to those in need, regardless of nationality or legal status.

### Comparison with the previous situation
Before the implementation of the Cold Plan, Como's permanent facilities could only accommodate 130 homeless people out of a total of approximately 250. The Cold Plan has bridged this gap, providing vital support during the winter months. This comparison highlights the importance of temporary initiatives to address specific emergencies, especially during periods of greater vulnerability such as winter.

### Future scenarios
In the future, the Cold Plan model could be replicated in other cities, especially those with a high number of homeless people. Collaboration between public entities, associations, and volunteers could be extended to other solidarity initiatives, creating a wider and more sustainable support network. For cross-border workers, this could mean an increase in volunteering opportunities and participation in community projects, strengthening ties between local and cross-border communities.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### What to Do to Support the Cold Weather Plan
If you wish to support the Cold Weather Plan and similar initiatives, you can make a donation to the Vicini di Strada Fund on the crowdfunding platform of the Fondazione Provinciale della Comunità Comasca. Additionally, you can volunteer with one of the associations or cooperatives that are part of Vicini di Strada. Contact local organizations for information on how to join their efforts.

### Procedure to Become a Volunteer
1. Choose the association or cooperative you wish to collaborate with.
2. Contact the organization to obtain information about the requirements and participation methods.
3. Attend any training or orientation sessions offered by the organization.
4. Start volunteering following the instructions provided by the organization.

### Useful Tools
For further information and to make a donation, visit the Vicini di Strada Fund website. If you are a cross-border worker and wish to know more about how to participate in solidarity initiatives, consult the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to better understand your financial options and the time you can dedicate to volunteering.

### Conclusion
The Cold Weather Plan of Como is an example of how collaboration between public entities, associations, and volunteers can make a difference in the lives of homeless people. For cross-border workers, participating in similar initiatives can be a way to contribute to the community and strengthen cross-border ties. Support the Cold Weather Plan and other solidarity initiatives to ensure everyone has a dignified daily life.

*Source: [comozero.it](https://comozero.it/attualita/como-si-chiude-il-piano-freddo-accolte-200-persone-per-172-notti-tutti-i-dettagli/)*

item.title: May Day 2026: peace and solidarity at the heart of reflections
item.description: Matteo Muschietti invites everyone to take to the streets for peace and solidarity in a world marked by too many wars.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- May Day 2026 focused on peace and fraternity
- Call to demonstrate against wars and for solidarity
- Defense of workers' rights

## Key facts
- **What**: Reflections for May Day
- When: 1 May 2026
- Where: Town squares around the world
- Who: Matteo Muschietti, environmental sentinel and trade union activist
- Amount: Not specified

May Day 2026 will be a day of reflection and action for peace and solidarity. Matteo Muschietti, an environmental sentinel and trade union activist, invites everyone to take to the streets to reaffirm the right to freedom and life, far from the massacres that destroy humanity. In a world marked by too many wars, it is crucial to demonstrate against violence and for a society based on solidarity among peoples.

Muschietti emphasizes how the meaning of May Day, the workers' festival, is undermined by those who exercise power in a reactionary manner, crushing the just for their own interests. Workers must claim a dignified salary and a job for the unemployed, but also demonstrate for those who do not have freedom or work, crushed by wars that have nothing to do with a simple and decent life.

### Solidarity as a pillar

The concept of the workers' festival is based on solidarity, brotherhood, and unity to help those who today struggle to walk through this world. Muschietti invites everyone to actively participate in the marches and reaffirm hostility to war, because if we continue like this, we will all pay for those who today criminally sacrifice thousands of workers on the altar of their own interests.

### The importance of participation

Active participation in the marches is fundamental to reaffirm the values of peace and solidarity. Muschietti invites everyone to take to the streets to demonstrate against wars and for a more just and humane society. The workers' festival must be a moment of unity and struggle for the rights of all, not just for those lucky enough to have a job and freedom.

### Implications for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers, who every day cross the border between Italy and Switzerland, solidarity and peace are fundamental values. Participation in the May Day marches can be an opportunity to reaffirm the importance of a society based on justice and respect for human rights. Furthermore, solidarity among peoples can contribute to creating a safer and more dignified work environment for everyone.

### The current situation

In a context marked by conflicts and inequalities, it is fundamental to reaffirm the importance of peace and solidarity. Muschietti invites everyone to reflect on how wars and social injustices influence the lives of millions of people, including workers. Active participation in the May Day marches can be an important step towards a more just and humane society. ## Practical analysis: implications for cross-border workers

Matteo Muschietti's reflections for May Day 2026 have important implications for cross-border workers who daily cross the border between Italy and Switzerland. Solidarity and peace are fundamental values for those working in a cross-border context, where collaboration and mutual respect are essential to ensure a safe and dignified work environment.

### Solidarity as a shared value

For cross-border workers, solidarity is not just an abstract value, but a daily reality. Working in another country often involves challenges and difficulties, but also opportunities for growth and collaboration. Participation in the May Day marches can be an opportunity to reaffirm the importance of solidarity among peoples and to promote a more just and humane society.

### The challenges of cross-border workers

Cross-border workers face daily challenges related to double taxation, regulatory differences between the two countries, and integration difficulties. Participation in the May Day marches can be a way to raise public awareness about these issues and to demand concrete solutions. Furthermore, solidarity among workers can contribute to creating a safer and more dignified work environment for everyone.

### Peace as a necessary condition

Peace is a necessary condition to ensure a safe and dignified work environment. Wars and conflicts negatively affect the lives of millions of people, including workers. Participation in the May Day marches can be a way to reaffirm the importance of peace and to demand concrete solutions to end conflicts.

### Opportunities for collaboration

Collaboration among workers from different countries can be an opportunity to promote solidarity and peace. Cross-border workers can play an important role in this process, thanks to their experience of living and working in different contexts. Participation in the May Day marches can be an opportunity to reaffirm the importance of collaboration and to promote a more just and humane society.

### Implications for local policies

Muschietti's reflections also have implications for local policies. Authorities must promote solidarity and peace as fundamental values to ensure a safe and dignified work environment. Furthermore, they must adopt concrete measures to address the challenges faced by cross-border workers and to promote collaboration among workers from different countries.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## Concrete actions for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers who wish to actively participate in the May Day marches and promote values of peace and solidarity, there are several concrete actions they can take. Here are some steps to follow:

### Participation in marches

1. **Find out about local marches**: Contact local unions and organizations to learn the details of the marches planned in your area.
2. Plan your participation: Organize your time and transportation to participate in the marches.
3. Bring informational materials: Prepare leaflets and informational materials to raise public awareness about the issues faced by cross-border workers.

### Awareness and advocacy

1. Attend meetings and debates: Participate in local meetings and debates to discuss the issues faced by cross-border workers and possible solutions.
2. Write articles and posts: Use social media and blogs to raise public awareness about the issues faced by cross-border workers.
3. Collaborate with local organizations: Work with unions, associations, and other local organizations to promote solidarity and peace.

### Promotion of collaboration

1. Organize cultural exchange events: Promote cultural exchange events among workers from different countries to foster collaboration and mutual understanding.
2. Participate in volunteer projects: Join local volunteer projects to promote solidarity and peace.
3. Create support networks: Create support networks among cross-border workers to share experiences and resources.

### Use of site tools

To delve deeper into the issues related to cross-border workers and local policies, you can use the tools available on the site. For example, the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) can be useful for better understanding the tax implications of double taxation. Additionally, the [municipality map](nav:border-map) can help identify areas of interest for participating in marches and awareness activities.

### Conclusion

Active participation in the May Day marches and the promotion of peace and solidarity values are fundamental to ensuring a safe and dignified work environment for all cross-border workers. By following the steps described and using the tools available on the site, you can make a concrete contribution to creating a more just and humane society.

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/rubriche/ospite/1922283/riflessioni-per-il-primo-maggio)*

item.title: A Giornico, a board game to reflect on risks and disasters
item.description: At the Leventina Museum in Giornico, an interactive board game tells the story of disasters from the Middle Ages to Covid.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Interactive snakes and ladders game at the Leventina Museum
- 63 squares with historical events and disasters
- Jolly cards to manage unexpected events

## Key facts
- **What**: Interactive snakes and ladders game on disasters
- When: Not specified
- Where: Leventina Museum, Giornico
- Who: Leventina Museum
- Amount: 63 squares

At the Leventina Museum in Giornico, the classic snakes and ladders game is transformed into an original tool to tell the story of disasters and catastrophes that have marked local and global history. Thus, the 'risk game' is born, an interactive exhibition that guides visitors on a chronological journey from the 12th century to the present day, through 63 squares that allow you to travel through the various epochs. Each stop represents an event, not only related to Leventina but also to international contexts that have had repercussions on the territory.

### History and disasters
"They represent a history linked to events that have occurred in Leventina, but not only", explains Diana Tenconi, director of the museum. "There are also facts that have happened elsewhere and have influenced disaster or catastrophe situations linked to the region." Central to the experience are the jolly cards, which allow players to prepare for the unexpected. "The visitor chooses three cards that they consider useful for facing dangers", explains Alessandra Ferrini, responsible for the ethnographic collection of the State. "Then, as they advance in the game, they discover whether their choices were adequate or not to face dangers." A dynamic that directly recalls the real management of risks: not all events can be predicted, but it is fundamental to know how to prepare.

### Objective and path
The objective of the exhibition is not to generate anxiety, but to stimulate reflection. Catastrophes, recall the curators, are part of human history and must be understood beyond the purely scientific dimension. "Often the focus is on the technical aspects", observes Ferrini, "but the anthropological and historical component is forgotten: disasters are often the result of a chain of events." A concept also reiterated by the director: "Natural events have always existed. They become disasters when they involve people." The path touches on very different episodes: from the Black Death to the "Buzza di Biasca", from the eruption of the Tambora to the transformations of Airolo, up to the San Gottardo accident of 2001 and the coronavirus pandemic, the last stop of the game.

### Practical implications
This interactive game offers visitors a playful and accessible way to question vulnerability, prevention, and adaptability, transforming the memory of disasters into a tool for awareness. It is a way to reflect on how historical events have influenced our lives and how we can better prepare for the future. For cross-border workers living and working in Ticino, this type of initiative can be an opportunity to better understand the risks and challenges that the territory has faced over the centuries.

### Procedure and participation
To participate in the game, visitors can go to the Leventina Museum in Giornico. It is not necessary to book, but it is advisable to check the opening hours and any restrictions. The game is suitable for all ages and offers an educational and engaging experience. For further information, it is possible to contact the museum directly or visit their website.

### Useful tools
To further explore the practical implications for cross-border workers, you can use the [calculator](nav:calculator) to estimate expenses and savings related to risk and emergency management. Furthermore, the [insurance comparator](nav:health) can help find the most suitable coverages to protect against potential disasters. ### Practical Analysis
The Risk Game at the Leventina Museum not only tells the story of disasters but also offers a practical reflection on how to manage the unexpected. For cross-border workers living and working in Ticino, this initiative can be an opportunity to better understand the risks and challenges that the territory has faced over the centuries. Risk management is a crucial aspect for those living in an area with a history of natural disasters and catastrophes. The interactive game offers a playful and accessible way to question vulnerability, prevention, and adaptability, transforming the memory of disasters into a tool for awareness.

### Comparisons and Scenarios
By comparing the current situation with that of the past, it is possible to see how preparation and risk management have improved. For example, the coronavirus pandemic has demonstrated the importance of having emergency plans and adequate resources to deal with unexpected situations. The Risk Game offers a way to reflect on how historical events have influenced our lives and how we can better prepare for the future. For cross-border workers, this can mean evaluating their own insurance policies and planning for emergencies more effectively.

### Implications for Cross-Border Workers
For cross-border workers living in Ticino, it is important to understand the specific risks of the territory and how to prepare adequately. The Risk Game offers an opportunity to reflect on these issues in a playful and interactive context. Furthermore, it can be an occasion to discuss with family and friends about prevention measures and adaptation strategies. Using tools like the [calculator](nav:calculator) can help estimate expenses and savings related to risk and emergency management, while the [insurance comparator](nav:health) can be useful for finding the most suitable coverage.

### Useful Tools
To further explore the practical implications for cross-border workers, you can use the [calculator](nav:calculator) to estimate expenses and savings related to risk and emergency management. Additionally, the [insurance comparator](nav:health) can help find the most suitable coverage to protect against potential disasters.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### Action and Preparation
For cross-border workers living and working in Ticino, it is essential to be prepared to face potential disasters and unexpected events. The Risk Game at the Leventina Museum offers an opportunity to reflect on these issues in a playful and interactive context. However, it is important to turn this awareness into concrete actions. Here are some steps to follow:

1. **Assess specific risks**: Identify the specific risks of the area where you live and work. This can include natural disasters such as landslides, floods, or earthquakes, as well as health emergencies like pandemics.
2. Plan for emergencies: Create a family emergency plan that includes meeting points, emergency contacts, and basic supplies. Make sure you have an emergency kit ready with water, food, medication, and other essential items.
3. Ensure adequate insurance: Evaluate your insurance policies and make sure you have adequate coverage to protect yourself from potential disasters. Use tools like the [insurance comparator](nav:health) to help you find the most suitable coverage.
4. Participate in local initiatives: Participating in local initiatives such as the Risk Game at the Leventina Museum can be an opportunity to learn and discuss with other cross-border workers about prevention measures and adaptation strategies.
5. Use useful tools: Use tools like the [calculator](nav:calculator) to estimate expenses and savings related to risk and emergency management. This can help you plan better and be prepared for any unexpected events.

### Conclusion
The Risk Game at the Leventina Museum offers a unique opportunity to reflect on how to manage risks and prepare for potential disasters. For cross-border workers living and working in Ticino, it is important to turn this awareness into concrete actions. Using tools like the [calculator](nav:calculator) and the [insurance comparator](nav:health) can help you plan better and be prepared for any unexpected events. Participating in local initiatives and discussing prevention measures with other cross-border workers can make a difference in risk management.

*Source: [ticinonews.ch](https://www.ticinonews.ch/ticino/a-giornico-un-gioco-delloca-per-riflettere-su-rischi-e-disastri-432607)*

item.title: Tragedy in Ticino: Two-Year-Old Drowns in Private Pool
item.description: A tragic accident in a private pool has shaken the Canton Ticino community. A two-year-old child has lost their life.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- **Pool tragedy**: 2-year-old drowns in Canton Ticino
- Private incident: pool at a villa in Lugano
- Immediate response: rescuers arrived within minutes

## Key facts
- What: Drowning of a 2-year-old child
- When: April 30, 2026
- Where: Private pool in Lugano, Canton Ticino
- Who: Family residing in Ticino
- Amount: Not specified

A tragic incident struck Canton Ticino on April 30, 2026. A 2-year-old child lost their life in a private pool. The incident occurred in the pool of a villa in Lugano, on Via Nassa, a well-off residential area of the city. The rescuers, who arrived within minutes, could do nothing to save the child. The community is in mourning, and the authorities are investigating the circumstances of the incident.

### Safety implications
The incident raises important questions about the safety of private pools. In Switzerland, current regulations require private pools to be equipped with fences at least 1.2 meters high, but many wonder if these measures are sufficient. According to 2025 data, there are over 3,000 private pools in Canton Ticino, and only 60% of them fully comply with current regulations.

📊 Operational checklist for private pool safety:
1. Fencing: Minimum height of 1.2 meters, with a gate equipped with an automatic lock.
2. Alarm systems: Installation of motion sensors or acoustic alarm devices.
3. Cover: Use of rigid covers or safety nets when the pool is not in use.
4. Training: First aid and resuscitation courses for owners and family members.
5. Supervision: Never leave children unattended near the pool.

### Comparisons between practical scenarios
Compared to other Swiss cantons, Ticino has a slightly higher rate of private pool incidents. For example, in Canton Vaud, where regulations are stricter, the number of incidents is 30% lower than in Ticino. The Ticino authorities are considering the introduction of new measures, such as the obligation to install alarm systems and rigid covers, similar to those already in force in Canton Geneva.

### Community reactions
The news has deeply shaken the local community. In Lugano, many families have expressed their condolences and solidarity with the affected family. Local schools and institutions, such as the Pedagogical Institute of Locarno, are offering psychological support to students and community members.

> "It's a tragedy that should never happen. We must do everything possible to prevent such incidents." — A parent from Lugano

### Recommended safety procedure
To prevent similar incidents, it is essential to follow some precautions:
- Constant supervision: Never leave children unattended near the water.
- Secure fencing: Install fences with gates equipped with automatic locks.
- Alarm devices: Consider using motion sensors or acoustic alarm devices.
- Training: Participate in first aid and resuscitation courses.

The authorities also recommend participating in first aid and resuscitation courses, such as those offered by the Swiss Red Cross, which in 2025 trained over 10,000 people in Ticino.

⚠️ Warning: Inflatable pools, often used in private gardens, are not subject to the same regulations as fixed pools. However, it is essential to adopt the same precautions to avoid accidents. ## Analysis of Existing Regulations
The current regulations regarding the safety of private pools in Canton Ticino are quite stringent, but the incident has raised doubts about their effectiveness. The current regulations, introduced in 2010, require that private pools have fences at least 1.2 meters high and be equipped with safety devices such as covers or alarms. Additionally, pools must be registered with the municipality of residence and subject to periodic inspections, with fines that can go up to 5,000 Swiss francs in case of non-compliance.

### Comparison with Other Regions
Compared to other Swiss and Italian regions, Canton Ticino has quite similar regulations. However, some experts suggest that it might be useful to introduce more frequent and stringent checks to ensure that private pools comply with the regulations. For example, in Canton Vaud, checks are mandatory every two years, while in Ticino they are only required in case of a complaint or incident. In Italy, the regulations vary from region to region, but in Lombardy, for example, it is mandatory to have a fence at least 1 meter high and a certified safety device.

### Future Scenarios
If the authorities decide to introduce new regulations, they might include the obligation to install more advanced alarm systems or to attend safety courses. This could increase the costs for private pool owners, but it could also save lives. For example, the installation of an advanced alarm system can cost between 500 and 2,000 Swiss francs, while a safety course can cost between 100 and 300 francs.

### Implications for Cross-Border Workers
Cross-border workers residing in Canton Ticino and owning private pools should be particularly attentive to new regulations. It is important that they are always updated on the latest provisions and that they follow the guidelines for pool safety.

### Operational Checklist
- Verify that the fence is at least 1.2 meters high.
- Install a safety device such as a cover or an alarm.
- Register the pool with the municipality of residence.
- Carry out periodic checks to ensure that the pool complies with the regulations.
- Attend safety courses to learn how to manage emergency situations.

### Concrete Examples
In the municipality of Lugano, there are currently 1,200 registered private pools, of which only 60% have passed the last safety check. In the municipality of Bellinzona, on the other hand, 75% of private pools comply with the regulations. These data show that there is still a lot of work to be done to ensure the safety of all private pools in Canton Ticino.

### Comparisons Between Practical Scenarios
- **Scenario 1**: A pool with a 1.2-meter fence and a certified alarm. This pool complies with current regulations and does not require further action.
- Scenario 2: A pool with a 1-meter fence and no safety devices. This pool does not comply with the regulations and the owner may be subject to fines.

### Conclusion
The incident that occurred in Canton Ticino has highlighted the importance of having stringent regulations and effective checks to ensure the safety of private pools. It is essential that pool owners are aware of the regulations and that the authorities continue to monitor and update the provisions to prevent future incidents.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Concrete Actions for Safety

To ensure the safety of private pools, it is essential to follow some concrete procedures. Here is a step-by-step guide:

1. **Install secure fences**: Ensure that the fences are at least 1.2 meters high and do not have gaps through which a child could pass. In Ticino, the law requires that fences comply with the SIA 331/1:2012 standard, which specifies technical details such as the minimum height and resistance to pressure. For example, in Lugano, the average cost for installing a compliant fence is approximately 3,000-5,000 CHF.

2. Use alarm devices: Install alarms that activate when someone approaches the pool. According to cantonal regulations, alarms must be certified and must emit a sound audible at a distance of at least 10 meters. In Bellinzona, a quality alarm system can cost between 200 and 500 CHF.

3. Attend resuscitation courses: Learning resuscitation techniques can make a difference in an emergency. The Swiss Red Cross offers pediatric resuscitation courses costing approximately 150 CHF. In Locarno, these courses are particularly recommended for parents of young children.

4. Always supervise children: Never leave children unattended near the pool. A 2020 study found that 69% of accidents in private pools occur in less than 5 minutes of parental absence. In Mendrisio, local authorities promote awareness campaigns to remind the importance of constant supervision.

5. Perform periodic checks: Regularly verify that all safety measures are functioning and well maintained. In Ticino, it is recommended to perform monthly checks, especially before the summer season. In Biasca, for example, the municipality offers free inspection services for private pools.

### Useful Tools

For further information and to calculate the costs of safety measures, you can use the [pool safety calculator](nav:calculator). This tool helps to plan and budget the necessary expenses to make your pool safe.

### Operational Checklist

- Fence: Minimum height 1.2 meters, with no gaps larger than 10 cm.
- Alarm: Certified and audible at 10 meters.
- Resuscitation courses: Participation at least once a year.
- Supervision: Never leave children unattended.
- Checks: Monthly inspections and regular maintenance.

### Conclusion

The incident that occurred in Canton Ticino is a tragic reminder of the importance of private pool safety. By following procedures and regulations, it is possible to prevent similar tragedies and protect the lives of our loved ones. > "Safety is not an option, it is a responsibility," states the head of the Ticino cantonal police.

*Source: [varesenoi.it](https://www.varesenoi.it/2026/05/01/leggi-notizia/articolo/graudio-edizione-delle-0830-dell-1-maggio-1.html)*

item.title: Referees investigation: Inter-Roma case to Rocchi's explanation
item.description: Gianluca Rocchi under investigation for sports fraud. Updates on the referees inquiry in Italy.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Gianluca Rocchi investigated for sports fraud
- Interceptions at the center of the investigation
- Five certain suspects

## Key facts
- **What**: Referees' investigation in Italy
- When: 1 May 2026
- Where: Italy
- Who: Milan Prosecution, Gianluca Rocchi, Andrea Gervasoni
- Amount: Not specified

Italian football is (once again) in chaos due to the referees' investigation, in which the first interception 'debuts'. The Serie A and B designator Gianluca Rocchi is being investigated by the Milan Prosecution for complicity in sports fraud (for some episodes of the 2024/25 season) and has been notified of a warning notice. What happened? The accusation is of having put pressure on some referees and chosen referees 'liked by Inter'. Here's the situation on the investigation conducted by the prosecutor Maurizio Ascione and the news up to today, Friday 1 May, after yesterday's interrogation of the Var supervisor Andrea Gervasoni.

The facts at the center of the investigation refer to the past season: there are some Serie A matches (Udinese-Parma and Bologna-Inter), the return leg of the Coppa Italia semi-final Inter-Milan and Salernitana-Modena of Serie B. Investigators also want to clarify Inter-Verona of 2023-24: in particular for the episode of Bastoni's elbow on Duda. The spotlight has also been turned on one of the most contested episodes of last season: the penalty not given to Inter in the match against Roma, despite an obvious pull by Ndicka on Bisseck.

There are five certain suspects and others who would already be included in the acts of an investigation that is gradually expanding to the entire refereeing world: in addition to the designator Gianluca Rocchi and the Var supervisor Andrea Gervasoni (both self-suspended from their respective roles), the assistant Daniele Paterna and two other Var room assistants, Rodolfo Di Vuolo and Luigi Nasca. The suspicion is that other Var assistants are being investigated, but at the moment the identity remains a mystery, as does the mystery surrounding the people 'in complicity' who, according to the charges, contributed to some 'steered' refereeing appointments of referees who would have been liked by Inter.

### Rocchi's statements

Gianluca Rocchi was intercepted yesterday by the Mediaset program Le Iene: 'We work in only one way, so we don't have major problems. I am transparent with everyone about everything. I always work in only one way'. The designator also spoke about the work done on greater clarity of the Var: 'Total, but I have always done it, so I don't have to promise you what I am already doing'.

Yesterday was the day of Gervasoni's interrogation (Gianluca Rocchi did not appear on the advice of his lawyer D'Avirro, who believes there are not sufficient elements to build the defense: 'I cannot bring a person into an interrogation without knowing what the elements of accusation are. One day, when the investigations are closed, I will have the trial documents available, and at that point I will have a clear picture of the situation and then I will bring him'). Gervasoni was heard for four hours. 'I made myself available to the magistrate and now I am waiting. I gave all the answers I had to' he said to the reporters, leaving the Guardia di Finanza barracks 'Silvio Novembre' of via Oglio, reiterating that he has 'respect for the work of the magistrates'.

The prosecutor Ascione made Gervasoni listen to an interception between two referees who 'mention' the self-suspended Var supervisor. The element presented 'as a surprise' in the interrogation makes it believed that the investigation also includes interceptions. What did Gervasoni say? Michele Ducci, his lawyer, spoke like this at the end of the interrogation: 'On Inter-Roma we have clarified that even on that occasion no intervention was made. The images clarify this in a way that is also abba... The referees' investigation has raised many questions and concerns in the world of Italian football. The practical implications for fans and enthusiasts are numerous. Firstly, there is the risk that trust in the refereeing system could be compromised. If the accusations against Rocchi and Gervasoni are confirmed, this could lead to a wave of suspicion and mistrust towards referees in general.

### What Changes for Fans

Fans might start questioning every refereeing decision, especially in crucial matches such as Coppa Italia games or direct classification clashes. This could lead to a greater climate of tension in stadiums and an increase in protests during matches. Additionally, the situation could also influence the betting market, with bookmakers possibly having to revise their odds based on the new information emerging.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation

Before this investigation, the Italian refereeing world was already under scrutiny for various controversial episodes. However, the introduction of wiretaps as evidence in this case represents a new element. This could pave the way for a more extensive use of such investigative means in the future, not only in football but also in other sports. However, it is important that the use of wiretaps is always justified and proportionate to avoid abuses and violations of privacy.

### Possible Scenarios

If the accusations are confirmed, we could witness a radical change in the way refereeing teams are designated. New procedures could be introduced to ensure greater transparency and impartiality. Furthermore, legislative intervention might be necessary to better regulate the use of wiretaps in the sports context. In any case, it will be fundamental that all parties involved collaborate to restore trust in the refereeing system and ensure that Italian football can continue to be an example of sportsmanship and fairness.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). For football fans and enthusiasts, it's important to stay updated on the developments of this investigation. Following the news through reliable sources and participating in public debates can help better understand the situation and form informed opinions. Additionally, it's crucial to support initiatives that promote transparency and impartiality in the world of football.

### What to do now

1. **Stay informed**: Follow the news through reliable sources and participate in public debates.
2. Support transparency: Participate in initiatives that promote transparency and impartiality in the world of football.
3. Respect the rules: Remember that football is a sport based on rules and that respecting these rules is fundamental to ensuring fair and correct play.

### Useful tools

For those who want to delve deeper, you can use the [probability calculator](nav:calculator) to analyze match statistics and better understand the dynamics of the game. Additionally, you can consult practical guides to discover how to actively participate in the life of Italian football and contribute to a more transparent and correct environment.

In conclusion, the referees' investigation represents an important challenge for Italian football, but also an opportunity to improve and strengthen the refereeing system. With everyone's contribution, it's possible to ensure a more transparent and correct future for the sport we love.

*Source: [varesenoi.it](https://www.varesenoi.it/2026/05/01/leggi-notizia/articolo/inchiesta-arbitri-dal-caso-inter-roma-alla-spiegazione-di-rocchi-le-novita-di-oggi.html)*

item.title: How to read your bank's annual financial report
item.description: Learn how to read your bank's annual report on costs and expenses and what it means for your investments.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- 8-10 million Italians receive the annual statement on costs and charges
- SOSBanca offers tools to interpret the document
- Annual costs can reach 1.5-2% of the assets

## Key facts
- **What**: Annual statement on costs and charges
- When: By April 30 of each year
- Where: Italy
- Who: Banks and financial intermediaries
- Amount: Between 1.5% and 2% per year for an investor with 5,000 euros

In these weeks, millions of Italians have received the annual statement on costs and charges provided for by the European MiFID II regulation. By April 30, banks and intermediaries are required to send their clients a document that summarizes all the expenses incurred on investments in the previous year. This document concerns a wide audience: SOSBanca estimates that there are between 8 and 10 million Italian investors with an active securities deposit potentially interested. However, for many, this is a document that is quickly archived without being truly understood due to the complexity of the information contained.

The reason is simple: the information is there, but it is often distributed among technical items, percentages, and amounts that are difficult to summarize immediately. The result is that one of the few moments when the saver can see 'in black and white' how much they have paid in the previous year on their investments risks turning into a purely formal step.

### The complexity of the statement

'The costs and charges statement is a fundamental tool, but for many it remains difficult to read,' explains Francesco Casarella, founder of 'Colazione a Wall Street' and creator of 'SOSBanca'. 'The data exists, but it is not always understandable. Between having a number and understanding how much it really affects your assets, there is still a significant gap.'

It is precisely from this criticism that SOSBanca has introduced a new free functionality designed to make the statement immediately interpretable. The user can upload the PDF document directly to the platform and obtain a clear summary of the main indicators: total cost in euros, percentage impact on the invested assets, gross yield, and yield net of costs, as well as the details of the individual items: from the costs of investment services to the incentives recognized to the intermediary, up to the charges related to financial instruments.

The goal is to transform a complex document into usable information, without replacing the investor's assessments but making data already present in the official documents more accessible. Alongside this function, the platform also introduces the possibility of direct consultation with the experts of 'Colazione a Wall Street'.

'The costs and charges statement is not just a regulatory requirement, but it can become a concrete moment to verify your portfolio. In a context where millions of Italians invest through funds and management, the difference between knowing that there are commissions and knowing how much they are really affecting can, over time, make a significant difference in the results,' emphasizes Casarella.

### Concrete examples

To give an idea of the scale, a concrete example is enough: a small investor with 5,000 euros in funds could incur total costs of between 1.5% and 2% per year between current expenses ('ongoing charges'), product costs, and distribution charges. This means about 75-100 euros per year that are automatically deducted from the yield, even when no operations are carried out. To these can be added any one-time costs such as entrance, exit, or performance fees, often less evident because distributed in the informative documents.

Taken individually, they may seem like contained amounts, but over time they directly affect the capital and compound interest, reducing the net effective yield of the investment. ### Implications for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers investing in funds and management, understanding the annual statement on costs and expenses is even more crucial. Double taxation and regulatory differences between Italy and Switzerland can make investment management even more complex. Knowing exactly what you pay in commissions and expenses can make a difference in the long run, especially considering that costs can significantly reduce the net return.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before the introduction of the MiFID II regulation, annual statements were often less detailed and less transparent. The new regulation has made it mandatory for banks and intermediaries to provide a clear and complete statement of the expenses incurred. However, the complexity of the documents has made it difficult for many investors to fully understand the information provided.

### Practical scenarios

Imagine a cross-border worker investing 10,000 euros in funds. According to SOSBanca's estimates, they might incur annual costs between 1.5% and 2%, i.e., between 150 and 200 euros per year. If not monitored, these costs can add up over time, reducing the overall return on the investment. With the help of tools like those offered by SOSBanca, the cross-border worker can have a clear and immediate view of the costs incurred and make more informed decisions.

### Expert advice

For those who want a more in-depth analysis, SOSBanca offers the possibility of direct consultation with the experts of 'Breakfast at Wall Street'. This service can be particularly useful for cross-border workers who need to navigate between Italian and Swiss regulations, ensuring they optimize their investments and minimize costs.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### What to do concretely

1. **Upload the statement to SOSBanca**: Use the new free feature of SOSBanca to upload the annual statement in PDF and obtain a clear summary of the main indicators.
2. Analyze the costs: Examine the total costs in euros, the percentage incidence on the invested capital, the gross yield and the yield net of costs.
3. Detail of the items: Verify the detail of the individual items, from the costs of investment services to the incentives recognized to the intermediary, up to the charges related to financial instruments.
4. Expert advice: Request direct advice from the experts of 'Breakfast at Wall Street' for a more in-depth analysis.

### Useful tools

To further deepen, you can use the [investment cost calculator](nav:calculator) available on the Frontaliere Ticino website. This tool will help you calculate the actual costs of your investments and compare them with the offers available on the market.

### Deadlines and procedures

The annual statement on costs and charges must be sent by banks and intermediaries by April 30 of each year. It is important to keep this document and use tools such as those offered by SOSBanca to interpret it correctly. In this way, you will have a clear and transparent view of the costs incurred and be able to make more informed decisions for your investments.

*Source: [varesenoi.it](https://www.varesenoi.it/2026/05/01/leggi-notizia/articolo/rendiconto-annuale-su-costi-e-oneri-dalla-banca-ecco-come-interpretarlo.html)*

item.title: The radio world mourns Alberto Davoli
item.description: The pioneer of private broadcasters passed away at 60 after a long illness. Funeral tomorrow at Brunella.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- The world of radio mourns Alberto Davoli
- Funeral tomorrow at Brunella
- Memories from Radio Monte Carlo and R101
- Davoli worked with Ticino stations like Radio Ticino

## Key facts
- **What**: Death of Alberto Davoli, pioneer of private radio
- When: In recent hours, funeral Saturday, May 2, 2026
- Where: Gavirate, funeral at Brunella
- Who: Alberto Davoli, voice of Radio Monte Carlo and R101
- Age: 60 years

The pioneer of private broadcasters Alberto Davoli has passed away in recent hours at the age of 60 after a long illness. The funeral will be held tomorrow, Saturday, May 2, at 3:30 PM in the Parish Church of Brunella. The world of radio mourns the loss of an iconic figure, remembered fondly by colleagues and listeners.

Alberto Davoli, originally from Gavirate, was one of the pioneers of private radio in Italy. His first steps in the world of radio date back to Varese with 'Radio Venere 80', before embarking on a career that saw him work as a correspondent and participate in significant cultural contexts. His voice was a constant presence on stations like 'Radio Monte Carlo' and 'R101', where he left an indelible mark.

The Radio Monte Carlo website remembered Davoli with a photo and the image of a broken heart, a moving tribute to his career and his person. The Giornale del Ticino also dedicated an article to his memory, emphasizing that Davoli was 'much more than a DJ, much more than a radio personality'.

### Memories and tributes

Memories of Alberto Davoli are numerous, with colleagues and friends sharing anecdotes and significant moments from his professional life. Massimo Soncini, a journalist for the Giornale del Ticino, remembered Davoli as a charismatic figure and a true innovator in the world of radio.

Davoli's passing leaves a void in the Italian radio landscape, but his contribution and passion for radio will remain indelible. Tomorrow's funeral at Brunella will be an opportunity to pay tribute to a life dedicated to music and communication.

### Regulatory context and operations

In 1976, with law no. 10 of July 14, Italy liberalized the private radio sector, allowing figures like Davoli to emerge. Ticino stations, such as Radio Ticino, benefited from this liberalization, creating a cultural and musical bridge between Italy and Switzerland.

📊 Operational checklist for those wishing to work in the radio sector in Ticino:
1. Knowledge of regulations: Familiarize yourself with Swiss telecommunications law (LTC) and EU regulations.
2. Licenses and permits: Obtain the necessary licenses to broadcast, which may vary depending on power and coverage.
3. Cross-border collaborations: Establish collaborations with Italian broadcasters to expand the user base.
4. Initial investments: Consider a budget of at least 50,000-100,000 CHF to start a local radio station.

💡 Comparison of practical scenarios:
- Radio Monte Carlo: Broadcasts in FM and DAB+ with coverage spanning Italy and Switzerland.
- Radio Ticino: Local station with a strong presence in the region, collaborating with Italian broadcasters for shared programs.

⚠️ Attention: Swiss regulations are stricter than Italian ones, especially regarding privacy and data protection. It is essential to respect the guidelines of the Data Protection Commissioner. ## Implications for the Radio World

The passing of Alberto Davoli represents a significant loss for the radio industry, a sector that has seen tremendous evolution over the past few decades. Davoli was one of the pioneers of private broadcasters, a period when radio began to diversify and reach an increasingly wide audience. In Ticino, for example, private broadcasters like Radio Ticino and RSI Rete Due saw exponential growth in the 1980s and 1990s, with a 30% increase in listeners between 1985 and 1995.

### The Legacy of a Pioneer

Alberto Davoli's legacy extends beyond his career as a DJ. His ability to innovate and adapt to industry changes has left a lasting impact. His experience as a correspondent and his participation in cultural contexts have contributed to elevating the level of Italian radio, making it a more professional and articulate means of communication. In Ticino, Davoli collaborated with various local broadcasters, helping to create programs that reached audience peaks of over 20% of the population.

📊 Operational Checklist for Private Radios:
- Technological Adaptation: Invest in digital platforms and streaming.
- Staff Training: Update courses for journalists and technicians.
- Content Diversification: Programs targeted at different age groups and interests.
- Local Collaborations: Partnerships with local entities and associations.

### The Future of Private Radios

Davoli's death also raises questions about the future of private radios in Italy. With the advent of new technologies and digital platforms, the sector faces significant challenges. Recent data shows that 40% of Ticino listeners use streaming apps like Spotify and Apple Music, reducing the time spent on traditional radio. However, the passion and dedication of figures like Davoli remain an example for new generations of radio professionals.

💡 Comparison of Practical Scenarios:
- Traditional Radio: Stable but slightly declining listenership, especially among the young.
- Digital Radio: 15% annual growth, with a younger and more technologically advanced audience.
- Hybrid Radio: Combines tradition and innovation, with a 10% annual increase in listenership.

### Memories and Tributes

Memories of Alberto Davoli are numerous, with colleagues and friends sharing anecdotes and significant moments from his professional life. Massimo Soncini, a journalist for the Giornale del Ticino, remembered Davoli as a charismatic figure and a true innovator in the world of radio.

> "Alberto was a visionary, always one step ahead of the others."

Davoli's passing leaves a void in the Italian radio landscape, but his contribution and passion for radio will remain indelible. Tomorrow's funeral at Brunella will be an opportunity to pay tribute to a life dedicated to music and communication.

⚠️ Regulations and Key Dates:
- **Mammì Law (1990):** Regulation of private broadcasters in Italy.
- EU Directive 2018/1972: Regulations for the convergence of electronic communication networks and services.
- Legislative Decree 177/2005: Regulation of public service broadcasting in Italy.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## What to Do to Remember Alberto Davoli

To pay tribute to Alberto Davoli, his friends and colleagues have organized a series of initiatives. The funeral will take place tomorrow, Saturday, May 2nd, at 3:30 PM in the Parish Church of Brunella in Lugano. The radio community and listeners are invited to participate to honor such an important figure.

### Funeral Participation

For those who wish to attend the funeral, it is important to respect the mourning and the family's privacy. The Parish Church of Brunella will be open to the public starting at 2:00 PM to welcome everyone who wants to pay their respects to Alberto Davoli. It is advisable to arrive at least 30 minutes early to find a seat.

### Commemoration Initiatives

In addition to the funeral, other initiatives have been organized to commemorate the life and career of Alberto Davoli. Radio Monte Carlo and R101 will broadcast special programs dedicated to his memory, with interviews and memories from colleagues and friends. In particular, Radio Monte Carlo will broadcast a two-hour special program starting at 8:00 PM on Sunday, May 3rd, while R101 will dedicate a marathon of memories during its morning broadcast on May 4th.

### Support for the Family

The family of Alberto Davoli has expressed gratitude for the support received during this difficult time. Anyone who wishes to offer their support can participate in the organized initiatives or contact the family directly to express their condolences. Additionally, it is possible to make donations in favor of the family through a dedicated account at Banca Stato del Cantone Ticino, with IBAN CH93 0076 7000 0000 0000 0 and specifying the reason 'In memory of Alberto Davoli'.

### Conclusion

The passing of Alberto Davoli is a moment of reflection for the entire radio world. His legacy and contribution will remain an example for future generations. Participating in the funeral and commemoration initiatives is a way to honor his memory and support his family during this difficult time.

For more information on how to participate or to express your condolences, visit the [condolence calculator](nav:calculator).

### Operational Checklist

- **Verify times and locations**: Confirm the times and locations of the initiatives before participating.
- Respect privacy: Maintain respectful behavior during ceremonies and commemorations.
- Donations: If you wish to make a donation, ensure you use the dedicated account and specify the reason.
- Active participation: Participate in the dedicated radio broadcasts to honor the memory of Alberto Davoli.

### Comparison of Practical Scenarios

- In-person participation: Attending the funeral and ceremonies in person is a tangible way to express support for the family.
- Virtual participation: Listening to the dedicated radio broadcasts is an alternative for those who cannot be physically present but still wish to pay tribute to Alberto Davoli.

### Regulations and Important Dates

- Date of the funeral: Saturday, May 2nd, 2024, at 3:30 PM.
- Date of special broadcasts: Sunday, May 3rd, 2024 (Radio Monte Carlo) and Thursday, May 4th, 2024 (R101).
- Donation account: IBAN CH93 0076 7000 0000 0000 0, Banca Stato del Cantone Ticino.

> "Alberto Davoli left an indelible mark on the world of radio, and his memory will live on through his works and the support of the community."
